title: Compound V fast-path review invocation receipt
description:
Canonical receipt (CR5-6/CR3-7) the dispatcher writes AFTER running the fast-path's combined SPEC+QUALITY review as an in-harness `needs_review` Task. The fast-path manifest carries only a review DECLARATION (fast_path.review:{backend:claude, tier:deep} OR model:opus); this receipt is the post-review PROOF that the declared reviewer actually ran and resolved to Claude Opus. The validator's two modes gate on it: `--mode pre-dispatch` FORBIDS a receipt (it cannot exist yet); `--mode post-review` REQUIRES + verifies it before REVIEWED/MERGED (CR4-1). Reviewer-opus is verified by resolving the declaration through the real resolver/config and requiring the concrete result == Claude Opus, with backend:claude required even when model:opus is pinned (CR5-5).

Binding: the receipt is bound to run_id, pre_eval_id, the manifest digest, the pinned pre-launch baseline SHA, and the FINAL diff digest — so a stale receipt from an earlier attempt cannot be replayed against a changed diff (it is invalidated before any re-review; stale-receipt replay is tested across resume). Written atomically. `digest` is the same canonical-JSON self-digest convention as the pre-eval record (compound-v-taxonomy.record_digest, exclude_field='digest').

run_id.description: The fast-path run this receipt belongs to.

pre_eval_id.description: The originating write-once pre_eval_id (the cross-artifact binding key).

manifest_digest.description: 'sha256:'+sha256(canonical bytes) of the run's manifest.yaml at review time — ties the receipt to the exact contract reviewed.

baseline_sha.description: The immutable PRE-LAUNCH baseline commit SHA the scope gate + F2 reclassifier ran against (never HEAD; CR5-3). Full 40-char hex.

final_diff_digest.description: 'sha256:'+sha256 of the canonicalized FINAL git diff (baseline..worktree) that was reviewed. A later re-review MUST invalidate this receipt if the diff digest changes — the anti-stale-replay guard.

reviewer_backend.description: MUST be claude (CR5-5): the reviewer⇒opus guarantee needs backend:claude even when model:opus is pinned, because tier:deep alone routes codex→gpt-5.6-sol, not opus.

reviewer_tier.description: The reviewer TIER the declaration resolved through — MUST be 'deep' (the combined SPEC+QUALITY fast-path review is always the deep reviewer). Recorded for a fuller seal; the reviewer⇒opus guarantee is carried by reviewer_backend:claude + reviewer_model (CR5-5).

reviewer_model.description: The CONCRETE model the declaration resolved to via compound-v-resolve-model.py against the project config. The validator requires this to be Claude Opus (a config override of models.<stance>.claude.deep that isn't opus fails, CR5-5).

worktree.description: The diff-root the producer hashed final_diff_digest against — the git worktree path the worker ran in. Recorded so the post-review validator recomputes the FINAL diff (baseline..worktree) against the SAME root the producer did, never a divergent one. Producer-trusted metadata (from the needs_review spec), NOT a reviewer-echoed binding field.

attempt_id.description: Which review attempt produced this receipt (increments on re-review). Lets resume distinguish a fresh receipt from a stale prior-attempt one.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the receipt was written.

verdict.description: Normalized combined SPEC+QUALITY verdict. `approved` is required to advance to MERGED; `issues`/`error` block the merge (fail-closed).

integration_rationale.description: The recorded rationale that the INTEGRATION pass is vacuous for a single-job fast-path (no cross-job seams) — an auto-pass WITH stated reason, never a silent skip (spec §4 review-pass matrix).

review_ref.description: Optional repo-relative path to the full review write-up. Advisory.

digest.description: REQUIRED self-integrity digest: 'sha256:'+sha256(canonical_json(receipt without `digest`)). An unsealed receipt (no digest) is schema-invalid AND rejected by the validator. Verified with compound-v-taxonomy.record_digest(receipt, exclude_field='digest') — the SAME primitive the producer seals it with, so producer and consumer agree.
